key_facts: log group /aws/lambda/checkout-service has no retention_in_days set
0.63 GB/month ingestion at $0.31/month — adding retention saves ongoing cost
fix: add retention_in_days to existing aws_cloudwatch_log_group block
data_loss_acknowledged = false — retention does not delete existing logs immediately
NAT Gateway nat-gw-legacy-batch processed only 4 KB/day over 21 days — confirmed idle
$33.48/month wasted on fixed NAT Gateway charge
NAT Gateways are stateless — data_loss_acknowledged = false
lifecycle prevent_destroy = false required on both aws_nat_gateway and aws_eip
associated EIP eipalloc-0c1d2e3f4a5b6c701 must also be released

decision_summary.rationale: Setting a retention policy is the correct action to prevent indefinite log accumulation.

/aws/lambda/checkout-service.technical_explanation: The CloudWatch log group for the checkout-service Lambda function has no retention policy, which means logs will accumulate indefinitely. Setting a retention policy is the standard practice to prevent excessive log storage costs and potential performance issues.

decision_summary.rationale: Destroying the NAT Gateway and associated EIP is the correct action to eliminate idle resource costs.

nat-0c1d2e3f4a5b6c701.technical_explanation: The NAT Gateway nat-gw-legacy-batch has been idle for 21 days, processing only 4 KB/day on average. NAT Gateways are stateless and can be safely destroyed when not in use. The associated EIP should also be released to avoid unnecessary costs.

cost_report.waste_evidence: NAT Gateway processed 4 KB/day average over 21 days — idle for 3 weeks.

risk_assessment.verification_steps: Verify that no resources in the VPC rely on this NAT Gateway for outbound internet access.

llm_response.group_summary: The findings include a CloudWatch log group without a retention policy and an idle NAT Gateway. Setting a retention policy for the log group will prevent indefinite log accumulation, and destroying the NAT Gateway and associated EIP will eliminate idle resource costs. The total monthly savings are $33.79, and the total annual savings are $405.48. The findings_actioned count is 2.

terraform_validate.name: terraform_validate
terraform_validate.details: Passed (undeclared-reference errors ignored — missing sibling resources are expected in eval workspace)

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure CloudWatch log groups retains logs for at least 1 year

raw.url: Add an api key '--bc-api-key <api-key>' to see more detailed insights via https://bridgecrew.cloud

nl_quality.name: nl_quality
nl_quality.details: The report covers core facts and uses specific metrics for the NAT Gateway, but misses several key details: the log group resource name (/aws/lambda/checkout-service) and the 0.63 GB/month ingestion figure are absent from the CloudWatch explanation, the rationale for both findings is generic rather than tied to the actual data, the lifecycle prevent_destroy requirement on aws_nat_gateway and aws_eip is unmentioned, and the CloudWatch savings figure ($0.31) conflates ingestion cost with retention savings without acknowledging that retention doesn't delete existing logs.
